title: Chore rotation spatial validation
rationale: Validates that chore-rotation NPCs navigate to the correct room tiles using the ASCII floor plan as the spatial reference. Haiku asserts NPC positions against the stable map glyphs (M/F/S/T) to confirm route correctness.

deliverables.description: Per-scenario validation: NPC positions vs chore-target fixture glyphs, route correctness against stable ASCII map.

acceptanceTests.assertion: All chore-assigned NPCs are within 2 tiles of their target fixture glyph (M microwave, F fridge, S sink, T toilet) on the stable map within 60 game-seconds of assignment.
No NPC traverses a wall tile (inner wall ─ │ ┌ ┐ └ ┘ ┬ ┴ ├ ┤ ┼ or outer boundary ═ ║) during navigation.
NPCs use door tiles (· open, + closed) as transit points between rooms; no NPC path crosses a non-door wall position.
Corridor floor tiles (░) show expected NPC transit traffic; office floor tiles ( ) show expected idle/working positions.

nonGoals: Do not validate non-chore NPC behaviours.
Do not generate new chore assignments.
Do not modify simulation config or source files.
